README.md:
# Wix MCP Shopping Server

**This server is for Wix stores. If your store is not on Wix, do not deploy this.**

Generated by Adeptra Merchant for **example-store.example.com**.

## What this is

An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that lets an AI shopping agent search
this store's product catalog and build a cart, using Wix's eCommerce REST
API. Its tools follow the same catalog/cart vocabulary your UCP manifest
declares.

## The hard boundary — read this first

**This server reads your catalog and builds carts. It never takes payment —
checkout stays on Wix.** The `begin_checkout` tool returns the current cart
plus a Wix-hosted checkout URL; the shopper (or the agent, on the shopper's
behalf) completes the purchase there, directly with Wix, through your
existing payment setup. Adeptra generated and configured this server — it
does not host it, and it never touches your money.

## About the permissions you're granting — read this before creating the OAuth app

Unlike a WooCommerce store (where the equivalent API is structurally
incapable of touching payment or admin data, no matter what), **Wix does not
currently offer a narrower permission scope than the ones below** — granting
this app the minimum scopes it needs still grants more than it uses:

| Scope | What Wix calls it | What this server actually uses it for |
|---|---|---|
| `SCOPE.STORES.PRODUCT_READ` | Read Stores products | Searching/reading your product catalog (read-only) |
| `SCOPE.STORES.CATALOG_READ_LIMITED` | Read v3 catalog | The one-time startup check confirming your site is on Catalog V3 |
| `SCOPE.DC-ECOM-MEGA.MANAGE-ECOM` | Manage eCommerce - all permissions | Building/reading the current visitor's own cart, ONLY |
| `SCOPE.DC-STORES.MANAGE-ORDERS` | Manage Orders | Creating a checkout object and its redirect URL, ONLY — this scope also nominally allows reading orders and creating fulfillments, which this server's code never does |

This server's code is the actual boundary: `src/wix.ts`'s header comment
lists every endpoint it calls, and explicitly, every endpoint it deliberately
never calls (order creation, payment submission, any catalog write, any
customer/order/settings read). Nothing in this codebase imports or
references a payment API. But because the grant itself is broader than the
usage, **treat your deployed server and its `WIX_CLIENT_ID` as sensitive** —
host it the way you'd host anything with elevated store access, not as a
disposable public script.

## Known gotcha: the Wix OAuth token endpoint is a Developer Preview API

The endpoint this server uses to get a visitor access token
(`POST /oauth2/token`) is currently marked "Developer Preview" in Wix's own
documentation, meaning Wix may change it. This is disclosed here, not hidden
— if authentication starts failing after a Wix platform update, this is the
first place to check.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 18 or later, on a host you control (a VPS, a small cloud instance,
  a container — anywhere that can run a long-lived Node process). Adeptra
  never hosts this for you.
- A Wix site with Wix Stores enabled, on Catalog V3 (see the gotcha below).

## Wix setup, step by step

1. **Create a Headless project.** In your Wix account, go to your site's
   dashboard and set up a Headless project (Wix's "Headless Settings" area —
   see [dev.wix.com/docs/go-headless](https://dev.wix.com/docs/go-headless)
   for the current path, since dashboard navigation can change).
2. **Make sure Wix Stores/eCommerce is enabled** on the site this project
   points at. If you're not sure, check your site's dashboard for the Stores
   app.
3. **Create an OAuth app for visitors.** Within your Headless project
   settings, create a new OAuth app scoped for visitor (anonymous shopper)
   access — Wix's docs title this flow "Create an OAuth App for Visitors and
   Members." We're not naming exact buttons here because dashboard wording
   changes; look for the OAuth app creation option in your Headless project
   settings.
4. **Grant ONLY the four permissions listed in the table above.** Do not
   grant anything related to customer data, site settings, billing, or
   marketing — this server needs none of it and never asks for it.
5. **Set the allowed domain** for the OAuth app to wherever you deploy this
   server (or leave it permissive during local testing, per your Headless
   project's own domain-restriction settings).
6. **Copy the Client ID** (Wix's docs also call this the "Client GUID") from
   your OAuth app's settings — you'll need it for `.env` below.

## Install

```bash
cd mcp-server
npm install
npm run build
```

## Configure

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and set `WIX_CLIENT_ID` (from step 6 above) and `WIX_SITE_URL`
to your real store URL. No client secret is needed or used — Wix's headless
OAuth flow for anonymous visitor sessions is a public-client flow (Client ID
only).

## Run

```bash
npm start
```

This starts the MCP server over stdio — the standard way MCP clients (agent
runtimes, UCP-speaking hosts) launch and talk to a local MCP server process.
On startup, it checks your site's catalog version first and exits with a
clear error if your site isn't on Catalog V3 (see the gotcha below) — it
will not fail confusingly deep inside a catalog call.

## Connect it to your UCP manifest

Once this server is deployed somewhere reachable, point your UCP manifest's
`dev.ucp.shopping` service endpoint at its deployed address (replacing the
placeholder Adeptra's manifest fix left there), so agents that discover your
manifest know where to reach this shopping server.

## Known gotcha: Catalog V1 vs V3

Wix Stores sites run on one of two catalog versions, V1 or V3, which are
**not** backward-compatible — and Wix has been migrating sites to V3
gradually (new sites first), so your site may still be on V1. This server
only supports Catalog V3 and checks for it automatically on startup, exiting
with a clear message ("This server requires Wix Catalog V3; your site is on
Catalog V1.") if your site isn't ready. If you hit that message: this isn't
something you can toggle yourself today — check with Wix support about your
site's catalog migration status, or contact Adeptra if you need V1 support
prioritized.

## Known gotcha: pre-headless stores may not expose all product fields

Stores created before Wix's headless features were enabled may not expose
every product field via the API without enabling specific site capabilities
first. If `search_products`/`get_product` return unexpectedly sparse data,
check your site's Headless project settings for any catalog-related
capability that needs enabling, and re-test with `curl` directly against
the Wix API using your access token before assuming this server is broken.

## You must complete

- [ ] Create the Wix Headless project + OAuth app (see "Wix setup" above).
- [ ] Grant only the minimum permissions listed above — no more.
- [ ] Set `WIX_CLIENT_ID` and `WIX_SITE_URL` in `mcp-server/.env`.
- [ ] Deploy this to a host you control (Node 18+) — Adeptra does not host it for you.
- [ ] Verify your site is on Catalog V3 and that product fields are exposed via the API (see the gotchas above) — the server's own startup check catches the catalog-version half of this for you.
- [ ] Point your UCP manifest's shopping service endpoint at this server's
      deployed address, then re-run your Adeptra analysis — that's what turns
      "cart/catalog/checkout capability enabled once deployed" into a genuine pass.

## Tools this server exposes

| Tool | Does |
|---|---|
| `search_products` | Search the catalog by keyword |
| `get_product` | Get one product's full details + variants by id |
| `add_to_cart` | Add a product (+ variant) to the cart |
| `update_cart_item` | Change a cart line item's quantity |
| `remove_cart_item` | Remove a cart line item |
| `get_cart` | Get current cart contents |
| `begin_checkout` | Return the cart + a Wix-hosted checkout URL (no payment) |
.env.example:
WIX_CLIENT_ID=REPLACE-WITH-YOUR-WIX-OAUTH-CLIENT-ID
WIX_SITE_URL=https://REPLACE-WITH-YOUR-STORE-URL.example

# No client secret — Wix's headless OAuth app flow for visitor/anonymous
# sessions is a public-client flow (Client ID only). See README.md for
# exactly how to create this OAuth app and which permissions to grant —
# read that before deploying; Wix's narrowest available scopes are
# broader than what this server actually uses.
